steps.title: Identify the two independent clauses requiring connection
steps.content: The sentence presents two related independent clauses: “She was not alone in her efforts” and “Fernando Orejuela and Stephanie Shonekan, among other scholars, also contributed to the project.” The transition word “however” must connect them.

steps.title: Apply the rule for punctuating a conjunctive adverb between independent clauses
steps.content: Two independent clauses joined by a conjunctive adverb require: IC; however, IC (semicolon before, comma after). When unavailable, a colon can introduce an elaborating clause: IC: however, IC. The second clause here elaborates on why she was not alone, making a colon appropriate. A incorrectly places a comma before and a semicolon after “however”; B omits all punctuation (creating a run-on); D mistakenly uses comma-however-comma. This matches choice C — C is correct.
